title: Buzzear vs Twittear
body: ![](http://www.google.com/s2/static/images/1444417344-GoogleBuzzLogo68.png) Google has 2 major tools, its search engine and [gmail](http://www.xarop.com/tag/gmail/), they are not social tools in the sense that they have failed to create experiences similar to those that [Twitter](http://www.xarop.com/tag/twitter/) or [Facebook](http://www.xarop.com/tag/facebook/) give us, they try with Orkut but it only works in Brazil and [Wave](http://www.xarop.com/donde-esta-google-wave/) has been a bluff since it's not what many expected. **[Buzz](http://www.google.com/buzz)** may achieve what they have been looking for, **make your Google profile more social**. They announced it just a few hours ago and it hasn't appeared in my gmail yet, but it does in the Google Maps on my [android](http://www.xarop.com/tag/android/) where you can sense its potential. At first glance it is another layer on the map that shows you the people around you and what they say ... It reminded me of [Foursquare](http://www.xarop.com/foursquare/), but I think it goes beyond that, they are different and complementary things. If on Twitter people tweet, on Buzz people buzzear. Buzzing, buzzear, rebuzzear, rebuzz, ... are going to be terms that we will have to add to our usual tags. **Do you think Buzz will be serious competition to Twitter or Facebook?**
